ud7.9:1.2:
on one occasion the Blessed One was wandering among the Mallans,
together with a large community of monks, and came to a brahman village of the Mallans named Thūna.
ud7.9:1.3: The brahman householders of Thūna heard that “Gotama the Sakyan contemplative, gone forth from the Sakyan clan,
ud7.9:1.4: is wandering among the Mallans together with a large community of monks, and has arrived at Thūna.”
ud7.9:1.5: So they filled the well all the way to the brim with grass & chaff, [thinking],
ud7.9:1.6: “Don’t let these shaven-headed contemplatives draw drinking water.”
ud7.9:2.1: Then the Blessed One, going down from the road, went to a certain tree, and on arrival sat down on a seat laid out.
ud7.9:2.2: Seated, he said to Ven. Ānanda:
ud7.9:2.3: “Please, Ānanda, fetch me some drinking water from that well.”
ud7.9:3.1: When this was said, Ven. Ānanda replied,
ud7.9:3.2:
“Just now, lord, the brahman householders of Thūna filled that
well all the way to the brim with grass & chaff, [thinking],
ud7.9:3.3: ‘Don’t let these shaven-headed contemplatives draw drinking water.’”
ud7.9:4.1:
A second time, the Blessed One said to Ven. Ānanda:
“Please, Ānanda, fetch me some drinking water from that well.”
A second time, Ven. Ānanda replied,
“Just now, lord, the brahman householders of Thūna filled that
well all the way to the brim with grass & chaff, [thinking],
‘Don’t let these shaven-headed contemplatives draw drinking water.’”
ud7.9:4.2: A third time, the Blessed One said to Ven. Ānanda:
ud7.9:4.3: “Please, Ānanda, fetch me some drinking water from that well.”
ud7.9:4.4:
Responding,
“As you say, lord,” to the Blessed One, Ven. Ānanda–taking a bowl–went to the well.
ud7.9:4.5:
As he was approaching the well, it expelled all the grass & chaff from its mouth and stood filled to the brim–
streaming, as it were–with pristine water, undisturbed & clear.
ud7.9:5.2:
“How amazing!
How astounding!–the great power & great might of the Tathāgata!–in that, while I was approaching the well,
ud7.9:5.3:
it expelled all the grass & chaff from its mouth and stood filled to the brim–
streaming, as it were–with pristine water, undisturbed & clear.”
ud7.9:5.4: Taking drinking water in his bowl, he went to the Blessed One and on arrival said,
ud7.9:5.5:
“How amazing, lord!
How astounding!–the great power & great might of the Tathāgata!–in that, while I was approaching the well,
ud7.9:5.6:
it expelled all the grass & chaff from its mouth and stood filled to the brim–
streaming, as it were–with pristine water, undisturbed & clear.
ud7.9:5.7:
Drink the water, O Blessed One!
Drink the water, O One-Well-Gone!”
ud7.9:6.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud7.9:7.3: Having cut craving by the root,
ud7.9:7.4: one would go about searching for what?
